title: pulmonaria mollis subsp. alpigena
intro: Pulmonaria mollis subsp. alpigena, also known as alpine lungwort, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the borage family (Boraginaceae) that is native to the mountainous regions of Europe. The plant's leaves and flowers have been used in traditional medicine for their anti-inflammatory and expectorant properties, making it a potential treatment for respiratory issues such as bronchitis and asthma. The health benefits of Pulmonaria mollis subsp. alpigena include reducing inflammation and relieving congestion, as well as its potential to boost the immune system. The plant can be prepared in various herbal forms, including teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are often used to support respiratory health and alleviate symptoms of colds and flu.
